pages.title: Best tools for creators
pages.summary: A practical mix for publishing, generation, research, and creative flow.

pages.title: Best MCPs to try
pages.summary: The MCP servers that are easiest to justify in a real builder workflow right now.

pages.title: Best APIs for automation
pages.summary: The model and platform APIs that matter when your workflows need structured output, tool use, and long-horizon reasoning.

pages.title: Best offline-friendly tools
pages.summary: Local or local-first tools that stay useful even when the browser tabs are closed.

pages.title: From the StackScout Lab
pages.summary: A clearly labelled subset of the tools we are building ourselves.

pages.title: Perplexity
pages.summary: Fast web-native AI research with a public-facing product direction that increasingly spills into APIs and device integrations.

pages.title: Hugging Face
pages.summary: The strongest public hub for models, datasets, Spaces, open-source research, and fast-moving AI tooling infrastructure.

pages.title: n8n
pages.summary: Workflow automation with a strong self-hosted story, useful AI integrations, and enough openness to stay flexible under real operator control.

pages.title: Make
pages.summary: A polished hosted automation platform that is shipping quickly into agents, credential collection, and MCP-shaped workflows.

pages.title: OpenAI API
pages.summary: A frontier model API stack that increasingly optimizes for real agent workflows, tool use, and professional work products.

pages.title: Anthropic API
pages.summary: A strong API lane for Claude-based reasoning, long context, and enterprise-facing agent workflows with a rapidly expanding model lineup.

pages.title: GitHub MCP Server
pages.summary: GitHub's official MCP server brings repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, and related workflows directly into agent loops.

pages.title: Playwright MCP
pages.summary: Structured browser automation for agents, backed by Playwright's accessibility-first interaction model and official docs.

pages.title: GitHub CLI
pages.summary: Still one of the best developer CLIs going, and now clearly leaning into agent skills and official extension ergonomics.

pages.title: Wrangler
pages.summary: Cloudflare's command-line surface for Workers keeps absorbing more platform breadth without losing its day-to-day usefulness.

pages.title: Canvas Planner
pages.summary: A visual planning surface for boards, campaign flow, and creative work without the drag of a generic project manager.

pages.title: Signal Stack
pages.summary: A publishing planner built around image, video, and queue workflows rather than generic social-media posting dashboards.

pages.title: Strudel Studio
pages.summary: A browser-based music studio path that brings programmable patterns, scenes, and samples into a clearer public-facing workflow.

pages.title: SyncPad
pages.summary: A private, desktop-first notes tool with live sync ambitions and a workflow that stays close to the operator.
